tweets.author: ownfeed.bsky.social
tweets.text: The largest US gold-backed ETF is down -13% since April, and the largest Bitcoin ETF is down -12% over the same period. Meanwhile, major semiconductor ETFs are up +81% and +60% respectively.

tweets.author: ownfeed.bsky.social
tweets.text: This trend is said to have accelerated in mid-May, with outflows from gold and Bitcoin funds more than tripling, while inflows into semiconductor ETFs have doubled.

tweets.author: ownfeed.bsky.social
tweets.text: Retail investors appear to be rotating out of gold and Bitcoin into semiconductor stocks. Since April, US gold and Bitcoin ETFs have seen -$12B in cumulative outflows, while semiconductor ETFs have attracted +$20B in inflows.
